original_hypothesis.title: Missing Deposit Window Enforcement Allows Risk-Free Sniping of Genesis Pools
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: `GenesisPool.depositToken` does not enforce the `NO_GENESIS_DEPOSIT_WINDOW` deadline on-chain. It relies entirely on an external keeper calling `checkBeforeEpochFlip` exactly 3 hours before the epoch ends to disable deposits. If the keeper is delayed or offline, attackers can deposit at the very last second before the epoch flip, observing the exact final valuation and sniping allocations risk-free.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: This breaks the economic fairness of the genesis pool, allowing sophisticated actors to extract value without taking the time-risk that early depositors took.

proof_of_signal.pattern: Missing check for block.timestamp < genesisInfo.startTime + genesisInfo.duration - BlackTimeLibrary.NO_GENESIS_DEPOSIT_WINDOW

preconditions: Keeper bot fails to call checkBeforeEpochFlip on time
Pool is in PRE_LISTING or PRE_LAUNCH state

initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Attacker waits until the last possible block to deposit
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Attacker gains optimal allocation with zero uncertainty, diluting early participants

economic_questions: How reliable is the keeper infrastructure?
What is the exact economic advantage of depositing last?
validation_requirements: Simulate keeper failure and measure late deposit impact on allocation ratios

preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: Arbitrage value of the native token vs funding token

validation_result.impact_summary: GenesisPool.depositToken lacks an on-chain check for the deposit window deadline, relying entirely on a keeper-triggered status change. If the keeper is delayed, attackers can deposit at the last second with perfect information, diluting early depositors.
validation_result.detailed_analysis:
The hypothesis correctly identifies that GenesisPool.depositToken (line 103-105) only checks `block.timestamp >= genesisInfo.startTime` but does NOT check the upper bound `block.timestamp < genesisInfo.startTime + genesisInfo.duration - NO_GENESIS_DEPOSIT_WINDOW`. The deposit window closing relies exclusively on the epochController calling checkBeforeEpochFlip, which transitions PRE_LAUNCH pools to PRE_LAUNCH_DEPOSIT_DISABLED. If this external call is delayed, deposits remain open past the intended cutoff.

The protocol is clearly aware of the NO_GENESIS_DEPOSIT_WINDOW constraint — it's used in both setStartTime() and approveGenesisPool() — yet depositToken itself does not enforce it. This is an architectural defect where a time-critical invariant is enforced by an off-chain component rather than on-chain logic.

With WEEK=1800 (30 minutes) and NO_GENESIS_DEPOSIT_WINDOW=600 (10 minutes), the window during which deposits should be blocked is a significant fraction of the epoch. A late depositor gains the advantage of observing the final allocation state before committing capital.
validation_result.code_evidence_assessment: GenesisPool.sol lines 103-105 clearly show only a lower-bound timestamp check. The missing upper-bound check is the defect. BlackTimeLibrary.sol line 8 defines NO_GENESIS_DEPOSIT_WINDOW=600. GenesisPoolManager.sol checkBeforeEpochFlip (lines ~210-235) is the only mechanism that transitions PRE_LAUNCH to PRE_LAUNCH_DEPOSIT_DISABLED, confirming the keeper dependency. Both setStartTime() (GenesisPool.sol line ~315) and approveGenesisPool() (GenesisPoolManager.sol line ~153) explicitly reference `startTime + duration - NO_GENESIS_DEPOSIT_WINDOW`, proving the protocol intends this constraint but fails to enforce it in the deposit path.

vulnerable_code_snippets.vulnerability_explanation: Only checks block.timestamp >= startTime (lower bound). Missing the upper bound check: block.timestamp < startTime + duration - NO_GENESIS_DEPOSIT_WINDOW. This allows deposits to continue past the intended cutoff if the keeper hasn't transitioned the pool status.

This is the ONLY mechanism to disable deposits for PRE_LAUNCH pools. It depends entirely on the epochController calling this function on time. If delayed or skipped, deposits remain open indefinitely until the next successful call.

calling_sequence: 5. NO CHECK for block.timestamp < startTime + duration - NO_GENESIS_DEPOSIT_WINDOW
6. Deposit proceeds, updating allocations and user balances
7. The missing check should have been enforced here but is instead deferred to checkBeforeEpochFlip
execution_path_analysis.vulnerable_code_path: The vulnerable path is: User deposits at any time after startTime as long as poolStatus is PRE_LISTING or PRE_LAUNCH. The intended restriction (NO_GENESIS_DEPOSIT_WINDOW before epoch end) is only enforced by checkBeforeEpochFlip changing poolStatus to PRE_LAUNCH_DEPOSIT_DISABLED. If the keeper is late (even by a single block), deposits can occur in the forbidden window. The attacker waits until the last possible moment in an epoch, observes the final allocation state, and deposits with complete knowledge of the outcome.

config_validation.deployment_evidence: No deployment scripts specifically configure the keeper timing or NO_GENESIS_DEPOSIT_WINDOW. The genesis-params.js script sets MIN_DURATION to 2*3600 and MATURITY_TIME to 900, but does not address deposit window enforcement.

validation_result.attack_scenario_validation: Attack scenario: 1) A genesis pool is in PRE_LAUNCH state. 2) The epoch is about to flip (within 600 seconds / 10 minutes). 3) The keeper has not yet called checkBeforeEpochFlip. 4) The attacker observes current totalDeposits and allocatedNativeAmount, knowing the exact exchange rate and allocation they will receive. 5) The attacker calls GenesisPoolManager.depositToken with a large amount. 6) The deposit succeeds because poolStatus is still PRE_LAUNCH and block.timestamp >= startTime. 7) At the epoch flip, checkAtEpochFlip launches the pool including the attacker's last-second deposit. 8) The attacker gains optimal allocation with zero price uncertainty, diluting early depositors who took on time-risk. With WEEK=1800 and NO_GENESIS_DEPOSIT_WINDOW=600, the forbidden window is 1/3 of the entire epoch — a significant attack surface.
validation_result.impact_assessment: MEDIUM severity. Early depositors who committed capital with uncertainty about the final allocation are diluted by late depositors who can observe the near-final state. The economic advantage is the elimination of time-risk: the attacker sees the exact allocation ratio and can calculate their return before committing. With fixed-price auctions (FixedAuction), the exchange rate is deterministic, but the allocation share depends on totalDeposits, which the late depositor can fully observe. The impact scales with the size of the late deposit relative to existing deposits.
recommended_mitigations: Add an on-chain upper bound check in GenesisPool.depositToken: require(block.timestamp < genesisInfo.startTime + genesisInfo.duration - BlackTimeLibrary.NO_GENESIS_DEPOSIT_WINDOW, 'DEPOSIT_WINDOW_CLOSED');

Alternatively, add the check relative to the epoch: require(block.timestamp < BlackTimeLibrary.epochNext(block.timestamp) - BlackTimeLibrary.NO_GENESIS_DEPOSIT_WINDOW, 'DEPOSIT_WINDOW_CLOSED'); for PRE_LAUNCH status pools
Implement the keeper call as a redundant mechanism rather than the sole enforcement point
validation_result.replication_plan: 1. Deploy GenesisPool + GenesisPoolManager + supporting contracts. 2. Create and approve a genesis pool. 3. Deposit enough to trigger PRE_LAUNCH status. 4. Advance time to within NO_GENESIS_DEPOSIT_WINDOW of epoch end (within last 600 seconds). 5. Do NOT call checkBeforeEpochFlip. 6. Call depositToken — verify it succeeds despite being in the forbidden window. 7. Compare allocation ratios of early vs late depositors to quantify dilution.
uncertainties: The actual keeper infrastructure reliability is unknown — if the keeper always calls checkBeforeEpochFlip with 100% reliability, the practical risk is reduced (but the code defect remains)
With WEEK=1800 (test/dev value), the window is 600/1800 = 33% of the epoch; in production with longer epochs, the relative window may differ
additional_testing_needed: Test with realistic epoch durations to measure the actual attack window
Simulate keeper latency scenarios (1 block late, 10 blocks late, full epoch missed)
Calculate exact dilution percentages for various late-deposit sizes
